description: Fix critical inaccuracies in user-facing documentation pages (quick-start, install, first-plugin) that would cause setup failures
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix Claude Code MCP config path and secret retrieval command in quick-start and install pages
userStories.description: As a new user, I want correct setup instructions so that my MCP client can connect to the OpenTabs server on the first try
acceptanceCriteria: quick-start.mdx references `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` instead of `~/.claude.json` for the Claude Code config file path
install/index.mdx references `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` instead of `~/.claude.json`
quick-start.mdx secret retrieval command uses `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` (includes --show-secret flag)
install/index.mdx secret retrieval command uses `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` (includes --show-secret flag)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two critical issues in the same two files:

1. Claude Code MCP config path: docs say `~/.claude.json` but the correct path is `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json`. See platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts line 167 which prints the correct path. Fix in:
   - docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx line 51
   - docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx line 43

2. Secret retrieval command: `opentabs config show --json` returns a masked secret (e.g., 'abcd...wxyz'). The --show-secret flag is required to get the full secret. See platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 53. Fix in:
   - docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx line 48
   - docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx line 40

Both are in the MCP client configuration code blocks.

userStories.title: Replace npm install -g with opentabs plugin install in quick-start and install pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to use the built-in plugin install command so that the server auto-discovers plugins without a manual restart
acceptanceCriteria: quick-start.mdx recommends `opentabs plugin install <name>` instead of `npm install -g opentabs-plugin-<name>`
quick-start.mdx does NOT tell users to restart the server after installing a plugin (the CLI auto-notifies the server)
install/index.mdx recommends `opentabs plugin install <name>` instead of `npm install -g opentabs-plugin-<name>`
install/index.mdx does NOT tell users to restart the server after installing a plugin
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The CLI has `opentabs plugin install <name>` (see platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts lines 697-711) which resolves the package, installs it, and auto-calls POST /reload to trigger server rediscovery. No restart needed.

Fix in:
- docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx lines 79-81 — change `npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack` to `opentabs plugin install slack` and remove 'Restart the server' instruction
- docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx lines 63-69 — same change, remove the restart instruction

userStories.title: Update first-plugin tutorial to match scaffold output
userStories.description: As a new plugin developer, I want the first-plugin tutorial code to align with what the scaffold generates so that I am not confused by differences
acceptanceCriteria: first-plugin.mdx src/index.ts example uses two separate import statements (import { OpenTabsPlugin } and import type { ToolDefinition }) instead of a combined import
first-plugin.mdx src/index.ts example uses `override readonly displayName` with the override keyword
first-plugin.mdx src/index.ts example shows isReady() returning false by default (matching the scaffold) with a comment about implementing real auth checking
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The scaffold in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts (lines 346-371) produces:
1. Two separate imports: `import { OpenTabsPlugin } from '...'` and `import type { ToolDefinition } from '...'`
2. `override readonly displayName` (with override keyword)
3. `isReady()` returns false by default

The tutorial in docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx (lines 56-73) has a combined import, no override keyword, and isReady() returning true.

Update the tutorial to match the scaffold output. The tool name `getPageTitle` vs `exampleTool` difference is fine since the tutorial explicitly says 'Replace the contents' — that's an intentional teaching choice. Focus on the structural differences that would cause TypeScript errors or confusion.
